steps.title: Identify what each choice is testing
steps.content: When a transversal crosses two lines, equal corresponding angles prove the two lines are parallel. Here $w=146^\circ$, so any sufficient statement must connect an angle at the other intersection to $w$.

steps.title: Check the sufficient statement
steps.content: In the diagram, $w$ and $y$ are corresponding angles: they occupy the same relative position where the transversal meets lines $r$ and $s$. If $y=146^\circ$, then $w=y=146^\circ$. Equal corresponding angles prove $r \parallel s$, so the statement $y=146^\circ$ is sufficient.

steps.title: Eliminate the other statements
steps.content:
• $x=34^\circ$ would make $w+x=180^\circ$. That only describes a linear pair at one intersection.
  • $w+y=180^\circ$ would make $y=34^\circ$ because $w=146^\circ$, so $y$ would not equal $w$.
  • $y+z=180^\circ$ only describes a linear pair at the other intersection.
Only B proves the two lines are parallel.
